flows.name: Connect WHOOP (OAuth Authorization Code + PKCE-less Confidential)
flows.purpose: Establish first-time WHOOP API access and persist tokens for transparent reuse.
flows.trigger: User taps 'Connect WHOOP' in Settings sheet.

steps.action: Generate CSRF state (UUID); store as pendingState.
Build authorization URL with client_id, redirect_uri (twoadays://oauth/callback), scopes (offline + read:*), state.
Open ASWebAuthenticationSession; user signs in to WHOOP.
Redirect to twoadays://oauth/callback?code=…&state=…
Validate returned state == pendingState (else throw stateMismatch).
POST authorization_code grant to /oauth/oauth2/token with code + client_secret.
Persist access_token, refresh_token, expires_at to Keychain (kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly).
isConnected = true. Fetch /user/profile/basic → cache to WhoopProfileCache.

edgeCases: User cancels ASWeb session → silent return, no error surfaced.
State mismatch → WhoopAuthError.stateMismatch (likely CSRF/replay).
Missing authorization code in callback → noAuthorizationCode error.
Token endpoint non-2xx → tokenExchangeFailed with HTTP status + body.

flows.name: Transparent Token Refresh
flows.purpose: Keep API calls authenticated without ever asking the user to reconnect for routine token expiry.
flows.trigger: Any of: (a) getValidAccessToken finds token within 5 min of expiry; (b) WhoopService receives 401; (c) scene becomes .active (warm-up).

steps.action: Load refresh_token from Keychain (else throw notConnected).
POST refresh_token grant to /oauth/oauth2/token with client_id + client_secret + scope.
Return new access_token (+ usually a rotated refresh_token).
Persist new tokens. If refresh_token omitted, keep existing (do NOT delete).

edgeCases: WHOOP returns 400/401 with invalid_grant in body → wipe Keychain, force reconnect.
Transient 5xx / network failure → tokens preserved, error surfaces, retry next time.
Refresh response omits refresh_token → existing token retained (defensive against partial rotations).
scenePhase warm-up failures are swallowed silently.

flows.name: Today Snapshot Refresh
flows.purpose: Populate Today card (recovery, HRV, RHR, strain, sleep) and Weekly card from live WHOOP data.

steps.action: WhoopTodayState.refresh(auth:) sets isLoading; spawns 3 concurrent calls.
GET /recovery, /cycle, /activity/sleep with 30-day window (paginated until next_token is nil or 200 records).
Each request uses Bearer access_token; 401 → forceRefresh → retry once.
Compute WhoopTodaySnapshot: latest day + weekly averages.
Persist snapshot to WhoopTodayCache (disk).
Render WhoopTodayCard + WhoopWeeklyCard; pick GREEN/YELLOW/RED rule by recovery score.

edgeCases: Not connected → snapshot shows cached value if any, otherwise empty.
5xx during pagination → exponential backoff (3 attempts) per request.
Decode failure → surfaced as errorMessage on state object.

flows.name: Daily Workout Logging
flows.purpose: Capture per-set weight + reps for the selected programme day; persist to SwiftData; reflect in History.

steps.action: Select day chip (Mon … Sun) via DayChipSelector.
loadInputs(): if a WorkoutLog already exists for today+day, pre-fill weight/reps fields.
Type weight and reps for each set across each exercise.
Tap 'Save Workout Log'.
Reuse existing WorkoutLog or create new one for (today ET, day).
Delete all child SetEntry rows; insert new ones for each non-empty (weight OR reps) cell.
ctx.save(); show '✓ Saved' for 2s; History list reorders automatically.

edgeCases: Empty sets (no weight AND no reps) are skipped — won't pollute history.
Same-day re-save is idempotent: prior sets cleared then rewritten.
Day is keyed by ET calendar to keep nights consistent regardless of phone timezone.

flows.name: Exercise Progress Drill-down
flows.purpose: Surface PRs and progression for a single exercise across all logs.
flows.trigger: User taps an exercise name in the Log tab.

steps.action: Tap exercise header → progressTarget set.
ExerciseStats.compute(for:from:) scans @Query allLogs to build PR + recent set summary.
ExerciseProgressView sheet shows PRLineView + history.

edgeCases: No history → empty-state copy.
Stats recompute reactively as SwiftData changes.

flows.name: Export Workout Logs
flows.purpose: Allow user to back up all logs as a JSON file via the iOS share sheet.

steps.action: DataExportService.encode(snapshot) → JSON Data.
Write Data to a temp .json file.
Present UIActivityViewController over the temp URL.
Save / share via system options.

edgeCases: No logs → Export button is disabled.
Encoding failure → exportError alert.

flows.name: Import Workout Logs (merge with dedup)
flows.purpose: Restore logs from a previously exported JSON without overwriting good data.

steps.action: Pick a .json via system file importer.
Decode ExportPayload; show confirmation alert with count.
Confirm import.
DataExportService.mergeImport: insert new logs; skip duplicates keyed by (date, exerciseName, setNumber).
Result alert shows imported vs skipped counts.

edgeCases: Invalid file → 'Could not read file' alert.
Dedup is conservative: exact (date+exercise+setNumber) match is skipped, partial matches are kept as new rows.

flows.name: Disconnect WHOOP
flows.purpose: Wipe all WHOOP credentials and caches on demand.

steps.action: Tap Disconnect.
Delete access_token, refresh_token, expires_at from Keychain.
Clear WhoopProfileCache and WhoopTodayCache.
isConnected = false → UI returns to Connect prompt.

edgeCases: Disconnect is silent (no confirm alert) — accepted because user explicitly tapped the row labeled 'Disconnect'.

supportingObjects.name: WhoopAuth
supportingObjects.role: Single source of truth for OAuth state, Keychain I/O, refresh logic.

supportingObjects.name: WhoopService
supportingObjects.role: Typed wrapper over WHOOP REST endpoints; handles pagination, retries, 401-driven refresh.

supportingObjects.name: WhoopTodayState
supportingObjects.role: ObservableObject feeding Today + Weekly cards from cached or freshly-fetched snapshot.

supportingObjects.name: WorkoutLog
supportingObjects.role: SwiftData @Model: one record per (date, day) with child SetEntry rows.

supportingObjects.name: SetEntry
supportingObjects.role: SwiftData @Model: a single set (exerciseName, setNumber, weight?, reps?).

supportingObjects.name: DataExportService
supportingObjects.role: Encode/decode logs JSON; merge with dedup on import.
